# 💬 SCBE-AETHERMOORE Public Forum - Questions & Discussions

Welcome to the SCBE-AETHERMOORE Community Forum
Ask questions, share insights, report issues
This page is for community discussion about SCBE-AETHERMOORE. Feel free to add your questions, use cases, or feedback below. We review regularly and update documentation based on your input.

📋 Frequently Asked Questions
General Questions
Q: What is SCBE-AETHERMOORE?  
A: A 14-layer post-quantum cryptographic system that uses hyperbolic geometry to verify AI intent and context. Think of it as "geometric trust walls" around your secrets.
Q: Is it production-ready?  
A: Yes! Version 5.0 is deployed on AWS Lambda with 226 passing tests. However, we recommend security audits for critical deployments.
Q: Is it really quantum-safe?  
A: Yes - uses NIST-approved Kyber (ML-KEM-768) and Dilithium (ML-DSA-65). The hyperbolic geometry adds extra defense layers beyond standard post-quantum crypto.
Q: What's the performance overhead?  
A: ~2ms total for encryption. Most layers take microseconds. Layer 9 (FFT) is the slowest at ~100μs.
Technical Questions
Q: How does the "fail-to-noise" feature work?  
A: Wrong context produces random-looking output instead of an error message. This prevents attackers from learning anything about why they were blocked.
Q: What's the "518,400× security multiplier"?  
A: It's the product of six Sacred Tongue weights (φ⁰ × φ¹ × φ² × φ³ × φ⁴ × φ⁵) when using full Roundtable governance (all 6 tongues). This represents compositional weight, not multiplicative cryptographic security.
Q: Can I use it with existing systems?  
A: Yes! It's designed as a drop-in encryption layer. Available as npm package (scbe-aethermoore) or Python module (install from GitHub).
Q: Does it work offline?  
A: Yes - edge deployment mode pre-computes lookup tables for offline operation. Trust scores sync when connection is available.
Implementation Questions
Q: Which layer should I focus on first?  
A: Start with Layer 5 (Hyperbolic Distance) - it's the immutable core. Everything else builds on that.
Q: Do I need all 14 layers?  
A: For full security, yes. But you can start with layers 1-5 for basic geometric trust verification.
Q: How do I integrate with AWS Lambda?  
A: See the deployment guide in 🚀 SCBE-AETHERMOORE Tech Deck - Complete Setup Guide. We provide a serverless.yml template.
Q: Can I customize the harmonic ratio R?  
A: Yes! Default is 1.5, but you can use φ² (≈2.618) for stronger walls. Configure in scbe.yaml.

🐛 Known Issues & Workarounds
Issue: Cold start on Lambda is 200-400ms  
Workaround: Enable provisioned concurrency (<50ms)
Issue: FFT in Layer 9 dominates latency  
Workaround: Use smaller FFT window (128 instead of 256 samples)
Issue: Windows native build has compilation errors  
Workaround: Use WSL2 with Ubuntu, or Docker
Report new issues on GitHub
